headline: Lin is running at a non-normal pace because decision bottlenecks and final reviews keep pulling them into default escalation.
summary_bullets: Lin linked the last stretch’s late nights to “carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews,” and said “the pace should not be normal.”
They explicitly called out a feedback gap: “I still do not get enough substantive feedback on key decisions.”
The fix Lin wants is ownership boundaries and delegation: “hand off one stream end-to-end and stay out of the middle.”
understanding_paragraph: Lin delivered strong outcomes, but the session clarified that the heavy pace is not just launch pressure—it’s sustained by decision bottlenecks and final-review work. Lin is also missing substantive feedback on key decisions, which reinforces them staying in the escalation path. The clearest new signal is that Lin already knows the operational change needed: end-to-end handoff and staying out of the middle.

axes.meaning: Running hot for multiple weeks; the pace is explicitly “should not be normal,” so this is actionable now.
Investment is being drained by bottlenecks and default escalation, not by lack of effort or outcomes.
Lin has a concrete direction for the next two weeks: hand off one stream and unblock the biggest dependency.
No clear learning/stretch signal; the growth dimension is dominated by process/ownership fixes.

brutal_truth_employee: Lin said, “I am proud of the outcomes, but the pace should not be normal.” They also named the mechanism: “Late nights came from carrying decision bottlenecks and final reviews,” and they’re asking for “clearer ownership boundaries so I am not default escalation.”
brutal_truth_manager: Lin gave a direct operational diagnosis (“decision bottlenecks and final reviews”), but the conversation didn’t lock down which specific decisions still require Lin vs can be delegated. Next time, push past “some of that work could have moved” and get to a concrete list of 3-5 decision types to reassign and a named owner for each.

next_actions.action: Schedule a 30-minute delegation mapping with Lin: list the decision bottlenecks and final-review steps, then assign an owner for each.
Confirm one end-to-end stream handoff plan with Lin and the team, including the decision-maker and what triggers escalation (and what does not).
Re-check with Lin: ask for the “one dependency” status and whether the late-night pace normalized; if not, name the new bottleneck.

watch_for: Within two weeks: if Lin still says “the pace should not be normal,” treat it as a delegation/ownership failure, not launch residue.
Before next 1:1: ask whether Lin is still “default escalation”; if yes, require a named ownership boundary change.
